nativeRange.habitat: Shallow coastal and estuarine environments; grows in sandy areas, seagrass beds, and around mangrove roots

avoidWith: tanks with no light schedule control

imageCandidates.notes: Mosaic Macros product image showing feathery frond detail.

summary: A feathery branching green macroalgae native to the Caribbean and Atlantic, widely regarded as a fast-growing, beginner-friendly display or refugium specimen. Allelopathic like all Caulerpa; banned in California.
careNotes:
Caulerpa sertularioides (Feather Caulerpa) is a siphonous green macroalgae featuring attractive fern-like fronds — feathery, oppositely-branched pinnules arising from a creeping stolon — with a pale to dark bluish-green coloration. The stolon anchors into sand, rubble, or rockwork via rhizoids. Fronds typically reach 5–35 cm in length depending on conditions and the age of the colony.

NOTE ON SCIENTIFIC NAME: The manifest for this entry lists 'Caulerpa sertularoides' (without the 'i') — this is a typographic error. The correct accepted name per Wikipedia and the taxonomic literature is Caulerpa sertularioides (S.G.Gmel.) M.Howe. This file uses the correct spelling.

Mosaic Macros is currently the primary hobby trade source for this species. AlgaeBarn and Top Shelf Aquatics do not appear to carry it at time of research. Husbandry parameters are extrapolated from genus-level Caulerpa standards corroborated by the Caulerpa prolifera sibling entry (sw-algae-004) and Wikipedia natural history data.

ALLELOPATHY: Like all Caulerpa species, C. sertularioides produces secondary metabolites including caulerpin and caulerpenyne. These compounds have been shown to inhibit or stress nearby corals and competing algae. It is generally advised to maintain physical separation from sensitive SPS corals and to consider running activated carbon in the system. Some keepers prefer refugium placement specifically to limit allelopathic exposure to display corals.

GOING SEXUAL / REPRODUCTIVE CRASH: Under stress — particularly nutrient crashes, disrupted photoperiod, or rapid temperature swings — Caulerpa species can undergo holocarpy, converting the entire thallus to gametes and effectively dying. This process releases organic matter and gametes into the water column, posing a severe acute water-quality risk (ammonia spike, oxygen depletion) capable of crashing a reef. Mitigations: run refugium lighting on a 24-hour cycle; harvest regularly to keep the colony in active vegetative growth; maintain stable moderate nutrient levels; immediately remove any specimen showing white/translucent fronds or turbid gamete release.

INVASIVE CONCERN: Caulerpa sertularioides has a wide tropical distribution and the genus includes several documented marine invasives (notably C. taxifolia in the Mediterranean). Captive specimens must never be released into natural waterways. The species is banned for sale in the state of California.

AQUARIUM CARE: Generally thrives under high reef lighting (5,000K+, 8+ hours photoperiod or 24-hour for refugium use). Tolerates moderate flow; gentle tumbling suits refugium deployment. Suitable for both display and refugium placement. Fast-growing; harvest regularly to maintain vegetative growth phase and prevent nutrient export from decaying old fronds.

UPON ARRIVAL: Fronds may appear white or translucent due to chloroplast displacement during dark shipping. This is normal and coloration typically recovers within hours of light exposure.
breedingNotes: Propagates readily via fragmentation — separated frond pieces have been reported to establish as new plants. Vegetative tip growth along the stolon is the primary growth mode in captivity. Sexual reproduction (holocarpy) is destructive and should be actively prevented (see careNotes).

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole known hobby trade source for this species at time of research. Product page confirms display suitability, feathery fern-like morphology, prolific growth, and California ban. Water parameters and general Caulerpa husbandry extrapolated from genus-level standards.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Caulerpa sertularioides
additional.notes: Confirmed accepted scientific name spelling (sertularioides, not sertularoides), taxonomy (Family Caulerpaceae, Order Bryopsidales), native range (Caribbean/Atlantic/Indo-Pacific), habitat (shallow sandy/seagrass/mangrove), depth range (0–10 m typical, sparse to 110 m), and physical dimensions (stolon fronds to ~0.35–2 m total thallus spread; branchlets 3–11 mm).

additional.name: Wikipedia — Caulerpa (genus)
additional.notes: Confirmed genus-level secondary metabolites (caulerpin, caulerpenyne, caulerpicin) and invasive designations. Notes C. sertularioides as 'Feather algae' documented at 11 m depth.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
